label: History of Capitalism & Labor

sources.title: History 393 – Slavery, Civil War, and Reconstruction
Foundations of Western Culture: The Making of the Modern World

app_prompts: History of capitalism timeline: user picks an era — mercantile capitalism, industrial capitalism, financialization — and traces how capital, labor, and the state related to each other.
Labor history case study: user picks a labor conflict (Homestead Strike, Triangle Shirtwaist, Flint Sit-Down) and traces what workers wanted, what employers did, what the state did, and what changed.

Racial capitalism explainer: user explores how racial hierarchy was integral to capital accumulation — slavery, convict leasing, racial wage differentials, redlining — not a distortion of capitalism but a feature.

Primitive accumulation case study: user picks an enclosure or dispossession — English commons, colonial land seizure, Native dispossession — and traces how pre-capitalist resources were converted into capital.

Financialization quiz: user reads about a financial instrument or crisis (mortgage-backed securities, private equity, credit default swaps) and traces how it connects to the real economy and who bore the risk.
